title: From Spoon to Spoils
theme: The reigning Wooden Spoon holder takes both pots, the defending Trophy champion takes the Spoon, and the man who should have closed the gap finds the 16th hole twice in the worst possible way.
narrative_structure: in_medias_res — open at Prince's R4 16th green, where Alex Baker has just signed for a 10 and the Trophy has effectively gone with it, then loop back to how the previously-Spooned David Mullin had built and rebuilt the cushion
opening_hook: R4, Prince's, the 16th — Alex Baker's second 10 of the week at the same hole number, a two-shot Trophy gap turning back into a four-shot lead — then rewind to a man who came into this TEG holding the Wooden Spoon
foreshadow: A reigning Wooden Spoon holder arriving in Kent having finished bottom in three of the last three TEGs
Alex Baker's blowups at par-5s and on the harder Cinque Ports stretch, hinting that the closer would find ways to give shots back
Jon Baker arriving as defending Trophy champion and back-to-back Jacket winner — and the only way that ends interestingly
The 16th hole as a recurring address on Alex Baker's scorecard

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Mullin took an outright lead at R1 H16 and was never headed across the next 56 holes. The cushion swelled to 9 after R2, held at 8 through R3, then was squeezed to 2 in R4 as Alex Baker piled up points around the turn-for-home — until Baker ran up a 10 at the 16th and handed the margin back. Mullin 154, Baker 152.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Mullin drew level on R1 H9, was outright leader by the close of R1, and stretched the Gross lead to 12 after R2 on the back of a new personal best at Littlestone (85). A quad at the 8th in R3 and an 8 at the 10th in R4 trimmed it, but the cushion was unrecoverable. +68, by 7 over Jon Baker — Mullin's 9th Jacket.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Jon dropped to last on R1 H4 and stayed there for all 68 remaining holes — bottom in every round. The defending Trophy champion's 92-92-91-83 told its own story: the R4 was a Prince's course record, but it arrived a day too late and 29 points behind the man above him. 125 points, his first Wooden Spoon.

headline_candidates: The Spoon Holder Sets His Stall
Mullin Plants the Flag at the 16th
rounds.chosen_headline: The Spoon Holder Sets His Stall
rounds.angle: Mullin shoots a new PB 87 at Royal Cinque Ports for 40 pts, draws level on the Jacket at H9, takes the Trophy outright at H16. Alex Baker bleeds early, runs up the first of two 10s at the par-5 16th. Jon Baker drops to last on H4 and stays there.

headline_candidates: Alex Baker Bleeds on the Front
rounds.angle: Mullin posts another personal best — 85 gross at Littlestone, 41 pts — to balloon the Trophy lead to 9 and the Jacket lead to 12. Alex Baker drops 12 shots across holes 2-5 and adds two more quads. The race, briefly, looks done.

headline_candidates: The Champ Wobbles, The Field Doesn't Pounce
A Quad at the Eighth, A Cushion Intact
rounds.chosen_headline: The Champ Wobbles, The Field Doesn't Pounce
rounds.angle: Mullin's first wobble — a 9 at the 8th — pares the Trophy lead from 9 to 8, no more. Williams posts a new Prince's PB (86) en route to what will become a Gross-total PB. Jon Baker still last.

headline_candidates: The Two-Shot Window That Closed Itself
rounds.angle: The squeeze: Alex Baker piles up 10 pts across H1-3, another 11 across H13-15, and the Trophy gap is briefly 2. Then a 10 at the 16th — same hole he found a 10 on in R1 — and the Trophy is Mullin's. Meanwhile Jon Baker shoots 83 to set a Prince's course record, his only round above 30 pts, and still finishes bottom.

players.arc: Arrives as reigning Wooden Spoon holder with the bottom finish in three of the last three TEGs; leaves with the Trophy and a 9th Green Jacket. Outright leader from R1 H16 to the final green, even through two blow-up holes.
The closer who almost was: trimmed a 9-point Trophy deficit to 2 by the back nine of R4, then handed it back with a 10 at the 16th — the second time he'd written that number on that hole this week.

Defending Trophy champion and back-to-back Jacket winner, bottom of the pile from R1 H4 onwards — and yet authored the week's most extraordinary individual round: an 83 at Prince's in R4, the lowest gross score that course has ever seen, four shots clear of his own previous Prince's best.
A career-best Gross total (+76) and new PBs at Royal Cinque Ports and Prince's — and yet, beaten on every metric that mattered. The man with three prior Trophies couldn't get into the conversation.

venue_notes: Second TEG visit to Kent (the first was TEG 13, last year, on the same four courses). Three classic links — Royal Cinque Ports, Littlestone, Prince's — and a 4th round revisit at Prince's. The whole field has played these layouts exactly once before, which makes the round-on-round deltas (Mullin -7 at Cinque Ports, -7 at Littlestone; Jon Baker +13 at Littlestone, +7 at Cinque Ports, then a course record at Prince's) directly comparable and unusually revealing.
course_history_notes: Mullin set personal bests on all three courses he revisited — RCP 87 (-7), Littlestone 85 (-7), Prince's 91 (-1) — the foundation of the double
Jon Baker was 13 shots worse than his TEG 13 visit at Littlestone and 7 shots worse at Cinque Ports — and then beat the entire course's record at Prince's by 2 in R4
Alex Baker's only personal best of the week was a 4-shot improvement at Royal Cinque Ports (98); he was 5 worse at Prince's vs last year
Williams's Gross-total PB (+76) was built on small improvements at every venue revisited — RCP -1, Prince's -3 — undone by an 11-shot regression at Littlestone
decisive_moments: R1 H16, Royal Cinque Ports — Mullin's par for outright Trophy lead; never headed across the remaining 56 holes
R1 H9, Royal Cinque Ports — Mullin draws level on the Gross and from there the Jacket gap only grows
R1 H4, Royal Cinque Ports — Jon Baker drops to last in the Spoon race on hole 4 of 72 and never climbs out

payoffs.seed: reigning Spoon holder, bottom in 3 of last 3
payoffs.resolves_in: Trophy + Jacket result, and the 'how the three were decided' section
payoffs.payoff: The same man takes the Trophy and the Jacket — the most complete reversal in a player's TEG career to date

payoffs.seed: Alex Baker's appetite for ruinous par-5s
payoffs.payoff: Two 10s at the par-5/par-4 16th — once in R1, again in R4 — the second one with the Trophy genuinely in reach

payoffs.seed: Jon Baker as defending Trophy and reigning Jacket holder
payoffs.resolves_in: Wooden Spoon section, and the R4 round write-up
payoffs.payoff: First-ever Wooden Spoon, despite a final-round 83 that is the lowest gross score ever recorded at Prince's

payoffs.seed: the 16th hole as Alex Baker's recurring address
payoffs.payoff: The second 10 at the 16th — same hole number, same scorecard — that ends the Trophy race
